                  android:theme="@style/UnityThemeSelector"

        </activity>
        <meta-data android:name="com.oculus.vr.focusaware" android:value="true" />
    </application>
</manifest>

    package="${applicationId}"

    <application
        android:usesCleartextTraffic="true"
        android:networkSecurityConfig="@xml/network_security_config">

</manifest>

<TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "8478e76c-1e59-468d-a36e-dc7af956648b", "args": {"code": "using UnityEngine;
using UnityEditor;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Reflection;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        var settingsPath = "Assets/XR/Settings/OpenXR Package Settings.asset";
        var packageSettings = AssetDatabase.LoadAssetAtPath<ScriptableObject>(settingsPath);
        
        if (packageSettings == null)
        {
            result.LogError("Could not find OpenXR Package Settings at {0}", settingsPath);
            return;
        }

        result.Log("Inspecting OpenXR Package Settings...");

        // OpenXRPackageSettings has a private list of Settings
        // We can get it via reflection or just use the serialized property
        SerializedObject so = new SerializedObject(packageSettings);
        SerializedProperty valuesProp = so.FindProperty("Values");

        if (valuesProp == null || !valuesProp.isArray)
        {
            result.LogError("Could not find 'Values' array in OpenXRPackageSettings");
            return;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < valuesProp.arraySize; i++)
        {
            var featureSettings = valuesProp.GetArrayElementAtIndex(i).objectReferenceValue;
            if (featureSettings == null) continue;

            SerializedObject featureSo = new SerializedObject(featureSettings);
            SerializedProperty featuresListProp = featureSo.FindProperty("features");

            if (featuresListProp == null || !featuresListProp.isArray) continue;

            result.Log("Checking platform settings: {0}", featureSettings.name);

            HashSet<string> seenFeatureIds = new HashSet<string>();
            List<int> indicesToRemove = new List<int>();

            for (int j = 0; j < featuresListProp.arraySize; j++)
            {
                var feature = featuresListProp.GetArrayElementAtIndex(j).objectReferenceValue;
                if (feature == null)
                {
                    indicesToRemove.Add(j);
                    continue;
                }

                SerializedObject featureObjSo = new SerializedObject(feature);
                string featureId = featureObjSo.FindProperty("featureIdInternal").stringValue;

                if (seenFeatureIds.Contains(featureId))
                {
                    result.LogWarning("Duplicate feature found: {0} (ID: {1}) at index {2}. Marking for removal.", feature.name, featureId, j);
                    indicesToRemove.Add(j);
                }
                else
                {
                    seenFeatureIds.Add(featureId);
                }
            }

            if (indicesToRemove.Count > 0)
            {
                // Remove from end to start to maintain indices
                for (int k = indicesToRemove.Count - 1; k >= 0; k--)
                {
                    featuresListProp.DeleteArrayElementAtIndex(indicesToRemove[k]);
                }
                featureSo.ApplyModifiedProperties();
                result.Log("Removed {0} duplicates from {1}", indicesToRemove.Count, featureSettings.name);
            }
        }

        AssetDatabase.SaveAssets();
        result.Log("Fixed OpenXR Package Settings.");
    }
}
", "title": "Fix duplicate OpenXR settings"}, "agent": "CoreAgent"}</TOOL_CALL>

<TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "8ec31882-bcb8-43f5-9d50-f2a678fe0fb7", "args": {"title": "Check and fix duplicate keys in Package Settings", "code": "using UnityEngine;
using UnityEditor;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Reflection;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        var settingsPath = "Assets/XR/Settings/OpenXR Package Settings.asset";
        var packageSettings = AssetDatabase.LoadMainAssetAtPath(settingsPath) as ScriptableObject;
        
        if (packageSettings == null)
        {
            result.LogError("Could not load OpenXRPackageSettings");
            return;
        }

        SerializedObject so = new SerializedObject(packageSettings);
        SerializedProperty keysProp = so.FindProperty("Keys");
        SerializedProperty valuesProp = so.FindProperty("Values");

        if (keysProp == null || valuesProp == null)
        {
            result.LogError("Missing Keys or Values property");
            return;
        }

        result.Log("Package Settings: Keys={0}, Values={1}", keysProp.arraySize, valuesProp.arraySize);

        // Check for duplicate keys
        HashSet<int> seenKeys = new HashSet<int>();
        List<int> duplicateIndices = new List<int>();

        // Keys is often a binary blob in some versions, or an array of ints.
        // Let's check the array first.
        if (keysProp.isArray)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < keysProp.arraySize; i++)
            {
                int key = keysProp.GetArrayElementAtIndex(i).intValue;
                if (seenKeys.Contains(key))
                {
                    result.LogWarning("Duplicate key found: {0} at index {1}", key, i);
                    duplicateIndices.Add(i);
                }
                else
                {
                    seenKeys.Add(key);
                }
            }
        }
        else
        {
            result.Log("Keys is not an array, type: " + keysProp.propertyType);
            // If it's not an array, it might be the binary layout.
        }

        if (duplicateIndices.Count > 0)
        {
            for (int i = duplicateIndices.Count - 1; i >= 0; i--)
            {
                int idx = duplicateIndices[i];
                keysProp.DeleteArrayElementAtIndex(idx);
                // Also delete from values
                if (idx < valuesProp.arraySize)
                    valuesProp.DeleteArrayElementAtIndex(idx);
            }
            so.ApplyModifiedProperties();
            result.Log("Deleted {0} duplicate platform entries.", duplicateIndices.Count);
        }

        AssetDatabase.SaveAssets();
    }
}
"}, "agent": "CoreAgent"}</TOOL_CALL>

<TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "50623bba-63b0-485f-ace6-2fc32ff0b9c7", "args": {"code": "using UnityEngine;
using UnityEditor;
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Reflection;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        try
        {
            // 1. Force find/create the OpenXRPackageSettings
            Type packageSettingsType = null;
            foreach (var assembly in AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies())
            {
                packageSettingsType = assembly.GetType("UnityEditor.XR.OpenXR.Features.OpenXRPackageSettings");
                if (packageSettingsType != null) break;
            }

            if (packageSettingsType == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not find OpenXRPackageSettings type");
                return;
            }

            var instanceProperty = packageSettingsType.GetProperty("Instance", BindingFlags.Static | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            var packageSettings = instanceProperty.GetValue(null) as ScriptableObject;

            if (packageSettings == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not get OpenXRPackageSettings instance");
                return;
            }

            // 2. Get Android settings (BuildTargetGroup.Android = 13)
            var getSettingsMethod = packageSettingsType.GetMethod("GetSettingsForBuildTargetGroup", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            var androidSettings = getSettingsMethod.Invoke(packageSettings, new object[] { BuildTargetGroup.Android }) as ScriptableObject;

            if (androidSettings == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not get Android OpenXR settings");
                return;
            }

            // 3. Find and Add Oculus Touch Controller Profile
            // We need to find the feature by its ID: com.unity.openxr.feature.input.oculustouch
            // or by its type
            Type featureType = null;
            foreach (var assembly in AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies())
            {
                featureType = assembly.GetType("UnityEngine.XR.OpenXR.Features.Interactions.OculusTouchControllerProfile");
                if (featureType != null) break;
            }

            if (featureType == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not find OculusTouchControllerProfile type");
                return;
            }

            // Use reflection to find the feature in the settings
            var featuresField = androidSettings.GetType().GetField("m_Features", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            if (featuresField == null)
            {
                 // Try 'features' property if m_Features is not found
                 featuresField = androidSettings.GetType().GetField("features", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            }

            var featuresList = featuresField.GetValue(androidSettings) as System.Collections.IList;
            object oculusFeature = null;

            foreach (var feature in featuresList)
            {
                if (feature.GetType() == featureType)
                {
                    oculusFeature = feature;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (oculusFeature != null)
            {
                var enabledField = oculusFeature.GetType().GetField("m_enabled", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic) 
                                   ?? oculusFeature.GetType().GetField("enabled", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
                enabledField.SetValue(oculusFeature, true);
                result.Log("Successfully enabled Oculus Touch Controller Profile.");
            }
            else
            {
                result.LogError("Oculus Touch Controller Profile feature not found in Android settings list.");
            }

            EditorUtility.SetDirty(androidSettings);
            AssetDatabase.SaveAssets();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            result.LogError("Error: " + ex.ToString());
        }
    }
}

<TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "bb0ba79c-3629-4fbe-983d-5d2dfbbce9a4", "args": {"title": "Enable Oculus Interaction Profiles robustly", "code": "using UnityEngine;
using UnityEditor;
using System;
using System.Linq;
using System.Reflection;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        try
        {
            // 1. Find OpenXRPackageSettings type
            var packageSettingsType = AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies()
                .SelectMany(a => a.GetTypes())
                .FirstOrDefault(t => t.FullName == "UnityEditor.XR.OpenXR.Features.OpenXRPackageSettings");

            if (packageSettingsType == null)
            {
                result.LogError("OpenXRPackageSettings type not found.");
                return;
            }

            var instanceProp = packageSettingsType.GetProperty("Instance", BindingFlags.Static | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            var packageSettings = instanceProp.GetValue(null) as ScriptableObject;

            if (packageSettings == null)
            {
                result.LogError("OpenXRPackageSettings instance is null.");
                return;
            }

            // 2. Get Android settings
            var getSettingsMethod = packageSettingsType.GetMethod("GetSettingsForBuildTargetGroup", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            var androidSettings = getSettingsMethod.Invoke(packageSettings, new object[] { BuildTargetGroup.Android }) as ScriptableObject;

            if (androidSettings == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Android OpenXR settings is null.");
                return;
            }

            // 3. Find Interaction Profiles
            var featuresProp = androidSettings.GetType().GetField("m_Features", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            if (featuresProp == null) featuresProp = androidSettings.GetType().GetField("features", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Public);
            
            var featuresList = featuresProp.GetValue(androidSettings) as System.Collections.IEnumerable;
            if (featuresList == null)
            {
                 result.LogError("Features list is null.");
                 return;
            }

            bool foundAny = false;
            foreach (var feature in featuresList)
            {
                if (feature == null) continue;
                string typeName = feature.GetType().Name;
                
                // We want OculusTouchControllerProfile or similar
                if (typeName.Contains("OculusTouchControllerProfile") || typeName.Contains("MetaQuestTouchProControllerProfile"))
                {
                    var enabledField = feature.GetType().GetField("m_enabled", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
                    if (enabledField != null)
                    {
                        enabledField.SetValue(feature, true);
                        result.Log("Enabled interaction profile: {0}", typeName);
                        foundAny = true;
                    }
                }
            }

            if (!foundAny)
            {
                result.LogWarning("No Oculus interaction profiles found in the list. Attempting to add via internal API...");
                // Some versions of OpenXR require adding the feature to the list first if it's missing
            }

            EditorUtility.SetDirty(androidSettings);
            AssetDatabase.SaveAssets();
            result.Log("Interaction profile update complete.");
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            result.LogError("Interaction Profile Fix Error: " + ex.ToString());
        }
    }
}
"}, "agent": "CoreAgent"}</TOOL_CALL>

using UnityEditor;
using System;

using System.Collections;
using System.Collections.Generic;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        try
        {
            // 1. Find the OpenXRPackageSettings type
            Type packageSettingsType = null;
            foreach (var assembly in AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies())
            {
                packageSettingsType = assembly.GetType("UnityEditor.XR.OpenXR.Features.OpenXRPackageSettings");
                if (packageSettingsType != null) break;
            }

            if (packageSettingsType == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not find OpenXRPackageSettings type");
                return;
            }

            // 2. Get the Instance
            var instanceProperty = packageSettingsType.GetProperty("Instance", BindingFlags.Static | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            var packageSettings = instanceProperty.GetValue(null) as ScriptableObject;

            if (packageSettings == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not get OpenXRPackageSettings Instance");
                return;
            }

            // 3. Get Android Settings
            var getSettingsMethod = packageSettingsType.GetMethod("GetSettingsForBuildTargetGroup", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            var androidSettings = getSettingsMethod.Invoke(packageSettings, new object[] { BuildTargetGroup.Android }) as ScriptableObject;

            if (androidSettings == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not get Android settings from OpenXRPackageSettings");
                return;
            }

            // 4. Find the Oculus Touch Controller Profile feature
            Type featureType = null;
            foreach (var assembly in AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies())
            {
                featureType = assembly.GetType("UnityEngine.XR.OpenXR.Features.Interactions.OculusTouchControllerProfile");
                if (featureType != null) break;
            }

            if (featureType == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not find OculusTouchControllerProfile type");
                return;
            }

            // 5. Access the features list
            var featuresField = androidSettings.GetType().GetField("m_Features", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic)
                                ?? androidSettings.GetType().GetField("features", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
            
            if (featuresField == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Could not find features field in OpenXRSettings");
                return;
            }

            var featuresList = featuresField.GetValue(androidSettings) as IList;
            if (featuresList == null)
            {
                result.LogError("Features list is null in OpenXRSettings");
                return;
            }

            object oculusFeature = null;
            foreach (var feature in featuresList)
            {
                if (feature != null && feature.GetType() == featureType)
                {
                    oculusFeature = feature;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (oculusFeature == null)
            {
                result.Log("OculusTouchControllerProfile not found in list. Adding it...");
                // Some features might need to be added. 
                // But usually OpenXR maintains a list of all available features and you just enable them.
            }
            else
            {
                var enabledField = oculusFeature.GetType().GetField("m_enabled", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
                if (enabledField != null)
                {
                    enabledField.SetValue(oculusFeature, true);
                    result.Log("Successfully enabled OculusTouchControllerProfile.");
                }
            }

            // Also check for Meta Quest Support feature
            Type questFeatureType = null;
            foreach (var assembly in AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies())
            {
                questFeatureType = assembly.GetType("UnityEngine.XR.OpenXR.Features.MetaQuestSupport.MetaQuestSupport");
                if (questFeatureType != null) break;
            }

            if (questFeatureType != null)
            {
                foreach (var feature in featuresList)
                {
                    if (feature != null && feature.GetType() == questFeatureType)
                    {
                        var enabledField = feature.GetType().GetField("m_enabled", BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic);
                        if (enabledField != null)
                        {
                            enabledField.SetValue(feature, true);
                            result.Log("Successfully enabled MetaQuestSupport.");
                        }
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            EditorUtility.SetDirty(androidSettings);
            AssetDatabase.SaveAssets();
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            result.LogError("Error: " + ex.Message);
        }
    }
}

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        GameObject ui = GameObject.Find("VRCourseSelectionUI");
        if (ui != null)
        {
            result.Log("VRCourseSelectionUI hierarchy:");
            LogChildren(ui.transform, 0, result);
        }
        else
        {
            result.Log("VRCourseSelectionUI not found.");
        }
    }

    void LogChildren(Transform t, int depth, ExecutionResult result)
    {
        string indent = new string(' ', depth * 2);
        result.Log("{0}- {1}", indent, t.name);
        foreach (Transform child in t)
        {
            LogChildren(child, depth + 1, result);
        }
    }
}
"}, "agent": "CoreAgent"}</TOOL_CALL>

The inactive root object with missing scripts is still the focus. I'm leaning toward removing the problematic components from the prefab `Lesson ListPanel`. It seems like deleting these missing script components directly on the prefab is the most straightforward solution. I should be able to script this removal for a clean fix.


", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT>

<TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "4bd32863-906e-4597-8747-709c92a7e4b5", "args": {"code": "using UnityEngine;
using UnityEditor;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        string[] prefabPaths = {
            "Assets/Prefabs/VRUI/LessonListPanel.prefab",
            "Assets/Prefabs/VRUI/CourseSelectionPanel.prefab"
        };

        foreach (var path in prefabPaths)
        {
            GameObject prefab = AssetDatabase.LoadAssetAtPath<GameObject>(path);
            if (prefab == null) continue;

            result.Log("Cleaning missing scripts on {0}", path);
            GameObject instance = PrefabUtility.LoadPrefabContents(path);
            
            int count = GameObjectUtility.RemoveMonoBehavioursWithMissingScript(instance);
            if (count > 0)
            {
                result.Log("Removed {0} missing scripts from {1}", count, path);
                PrefabUtility.SaveAsPrefabAsset(instance, path);
            }
            PrefabUtility.UnloadPrefabContents(instance);
        }

        // Also clean the scene objects
        var all = Resources.FindObjectsOfTypeAll<GameObject>();
        foreach (var go in all)
        {
            if (go.hideFlags == HideFlags.None && (go.name == "LessonListPanel" || go.name == "CourseSelectionPanel"))
            {
                int count = GameObjectUtility.RemoveMonoBehavioursWithMissingScript(go);
                if (count > 0)
                {
                    result.Log("Removed {0} missing scripts from scene object {1}", count, go.name);
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
", "title": "Remove missing scripts from prefabs and scene objects"}, "agent": "CoreAgent"}</TOOL_CALL>

<THOUGHT>{"content": "**Examining Build Warnings**

I'm currently focused on the persistent warnings during the "Build and Run" process. The "Latency Optimization" message suggesting "Prioritize Input Polling" keeps popping up. Also, I've noted a large number of shader warnings, but, as they stem from the `Sentis` package, I'm inclined to dismiss them for now. I'm prioritizing the former message, which feels more important.


", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT>

<THOUGHT>{"content": "**Analyzing OpenXR Settings**

I've confirmed the "Prioritize Input Polling" setting corresponds to a value of `1`. Now, I'm ready to modify the `OpenXRPackageSettings.asset` file for the Android platform. Specifically, I'll update the `m_latencyOptimization` parameter within the `Android` OpenXR settings. The shader warnings persist, of course, but as they don't seem to impact the build process, they can be revisited later.


", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT>

<THOUGHT>{"content": "**Adjusting Android OpenXR**

I've located the `m_latencyOptimization` parameter within the `Android` settings and see it's currently set to `0`. Confirming that 'Prioritize Input Polling' corresponds to a value of `1`, I'm ready to update `OpenXRPackageSettings.asset`. I've also re-confirmed those shader warnings remain and will be ignored for now. My next step will be to confirm the changes have been applied in my next build.

payload.markdown:
<THOUGHT>{"content": "**Evaluating Floor Existence**

I'm currently verifying the absence of a pre-existing floor object within the current scene. If no suitable floor exists, I intend to create a plane, ensuring its scale and position align with VR requirements. I'll utilize `Unity.RunCommand` to handle the instantiation. I am leaning towards checking for objects named "Floor" before proceeding.


", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT>

<TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "c773e9de-f82e-4df4-8a55-f09ebdbea14f", "args": {"code": "using UnityEngine;
using UnityEditor;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        GameObject floor = GameObject.Find("Floor");
        if (floor != null)
        {
            result.Log("A floor already exists: {0}", floor.name);
            return;
        }

        // Create a plane for the floor
        GameObject plane = GameObject.CreatePrimitive(PrimitiveType.Plane);
        plane.name = "Floor";
        plane.transform.position = Vector3.zero;
        plane.transform.localScale = new Vector3(5, 1, 5); // 50x50 meters

        // Give it a simple material if possible, or just leave as default
        MeshRenderer renderer = plane.GetComponent<MeshRenderer>();
        if (renderer != null)
        {
            // Try to find a standard floor material or create a subtle dark one
            Material floorMat = new Material(Shader.Find("Universal Render Pipeline/Lit"));
            floorMat.color = new Color(0.2f, 0.2f, 0.2f);
            renderer.sharedMaterial = floorMat;
            
            // Note: In a real project, we'd save this material to Assets, 
            // but for a quick scene addition, sharedMaterial with a new instance is okay for now,
            // though it might not persist across sessions if not saved.
            // Let's just use the default for better persistence unless we create an asset.
        }

        result.RegisterObjectCreation(plane);
        result.Log("Created a floor (Plane) at (0,0,0) with scale (5,1,5).");
    }
}
", "title": "Add floor to scene"}, "agent": "CoreAgent"}</TOOL_CALL>
